skill.name: salesforce-development
skill.description: Expert patterns for Salesforce platform development including Lightning Web Components (LWC), Apex triggers and classes, REST/Bulk APIs, Connected Apps, and Salesforce DX with scratch orgs and 2nd generation packages (2GP). Use when: salesforce, sfdc, apex, lwc, lightning web components.
skill.summary: Guides Salesforce development patterns for LWC, Apex, APIs, Connected Apps, and Salesforce DX.
skill.author: sickn33

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm

content.user_title: Build Salesforce Apps with Apex and LWC
content.value_statement: Salesforce teams need consistent patterns for Apex, Lightning Web Components, APIs, and release workflows. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for platform-specific implementation decisions.
actual_capabilities: Guides Lightning Web Component patterns using wire service and reactive data binding.
Explains bulkified Apex trigger design with handler classes and recursion prevention.
Recommends Queueable Apex for asynchronous processing and job chaining limits.
Highlights Salesforce anti-patterns such as SOQL, DML, and hardcoded IDs in loops.
Covers Salesforce platform areas including REST APIs, Bulk APIs, Connected Apps, scratch orgs, and 2GP.
limitations: Does not include runnable Salesforce project files or generated Apex classes.
Does not authenticate to Salesforce orgs or execute Salesforce CLI commands.
Does not replace Salesforce security review or production release approval.
Contains brief guidance and requires project context for detailed architecture decisions.

use_cases.title: Design an Apex Trigger
use_cases.description: Plan a bulkified trigger and handler structure before implementing record automation.

use_cases.title: Review an LWC Data Pattern
use_cases.description: Check whether a component should use wire service, Lightning Data Service, or Apex methods.

use_cases.title: Prepare a DX Release Workflow
use_cases.description: Use scratch org and 2GP guidance when planning Salesforce package delivery.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain when to use Salesforce wire service, Apex methods, and Lightning Data Service for an LWC feature.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Trigger
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a bulkified Apex trigger handler for this object change. Include recursion prevention and test considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Review Async Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Salesforce async requirement and recommend whether Queueable Apex fits. Include limits and failure handling.

prompt_templates.title: Assess a Delivery Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Salesforce DX and 2GP delivery plan for this feature set, including scratch org usage and packaging risks.

output_examples.input: How should I structure an account update trigger?
output_examples.output: A concise trigger-handler plan that separates trigger entry, business logic, recursion control, and unit test coverage.

output_examples.input: Should this LWC call Apex directly?
output_examples.output: A recommendation comparing wire service, Lightning Data Service, and Apex methods based on data shape and reactivity.

output_examples.input: Can Queueable Apex handle this integration step?
output_examples.output: A limit-aware assessment covering queueable payloads, monitoring, chaining, and transaction boundaries.

best_practices: Provide the Salesforce object model, trigger events, and governor-limit concerns before asking for implementation guidance.
Ask for test strategy with every Apex design so bulk behavior and failure paths are covered.
Validate generated recommendations against current Salesforce org settings and release policies.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for single-record Apex logic when production transactions may process many records.
Do not hardcode Salesforce record IDs, profile IDs, or environment-specific values.
Do not treat brief pattern guidance as a complete security or compliance review.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete Salesforce projects?
faq.answer: No. It provides development patterns and review guidance for Salesforce implementation work.

faq.question: Can it help with Lightning Web Components?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers LWC data access patterns, especially wire service and reactive data binding.

faq.question: Can it review Apex trigger designs?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on bulkification, handler separation, recursion prevention, and testability.

faq.question: Does it connect to my Salesforce org?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not authenticate, run CLI commands, or access org data.

faq.question: Is it useful for Salesforce DX?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for scratch orgs and second-generation package workflows.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
